applicability: All organisations seeking or maintaining ISO/IEC 27001 certification or implementing an Information Security Management System

controls.title: Information Security Policies
controls.description: Information security policies must be defined, approved, published, and reviewed at planned intervals.
requiredEvidence: Information security policy requirement documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Security policy governance referenced in SRS constraint requirements

controls.title: Information Classification
controls.description: Information must be classified in terms of legal requirements, value, and sensitivity to unauthorised disclosure or modification.
requiredEvidence: Information classification scheme documented in SDS Security Architecture — Data Protection subsection
Classification levels and handling rules documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)

controls.title: Access Control Policy
controls.description: Access to information and systems must be restricted based on business and security requirements through a formal access control policy.
requiredEvidence: Access control policy and RBAC model documented in SDS Security Architecture — Authorization subsection
Least privilege and need-to-know requirements documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)

controls.title: Cryptography Policy
controls.description: A policy on the use of cryptographic controls must be developed and implemented to protect information.
requiredEvidence: Cryptography policy and approved algorithms documented in SDS Security Architecture — Data Protection subsection
Key management lifecycle documented in SDS Security Architecture section
Encryption requirements documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)

controls.title: Secure Development Lifecycle
controls.description: Rules for secure development of software and systems must be established and applied to within-organisation development activities.
requiredEvidence: Secure development lifecycle requirement documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Security testing requirements documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Code review and SAST process referenced in SDS Component Specifications section

controls.title: System Acquisition Security Requirements
controls.description: Information security requirements must be included in requirements for new information systems or enhancements to existing systems.
requiredEvidence: Information security requirements documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*) with explicit ISO 27001 reference
Security design requirements included in SDS Security Architecture section

controls.title: Protection of Test Data
controls.description: Test data must be selected carefully, protected, and controlled to avoid exposure of production personal or sensitive data in test environments.
requiredEvidence: Test data protection requirement documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Data masking or synthetic data generation approach referenced in SDS Component Specifications section

controls.title: Monitoring and Logging
controls.description: Systems must be monitored and security events logged with logs protected from tampering and retained for the required period.
requiredEvidence: Logging and monitoring requirements documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*) with retention periods
Audit logging component described in SDS Security Architecture — Audit Logging subsection
Log integrity protection mechanism referenced in SDS Security Architecture section

controls.title: Vulnerability Management
controls.description: Technical vulnerabilities in systems must be identified, evaluated, and treated in a timely manner.
requiredEvidence: Vulnerability management process documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*) with remediation timelines
Vulnerability scanning requirement documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Technology stack version management referenced in SDS Component Specifications section

controls.title: Supplier Relationships Security
controls.description: Requirements for addressing information security within supplier relationships must be agreed and documented.
requiredEvidence: Supplier security requirements documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Third-party vendor security requirements referenced in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Information Security Incident Management
controls.description: Responsibilities and procedures must be established to ensure a quick, effective, and orderly response to information security incidents.
requiredEvidence: Security incident management procedure documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Incident detection and escalation mechanism described in SDS Security Architecture section

controls.title: Network Security Controls
controls.description: Networks must be managed and controlled to protect information in systems and applications.
requiredEvidence: Network security controls described in SDS Architecture Overview section
Firewall and segmentation requirements documented in SDS Security Architecture section
Network monitoring requirements referenced in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)

controls.title: Business Continuity Information Security
controls.description: Information security continuity must be embedded in the organisation's business continuity management systems.
requiredEvidence: Business continuity and availability requirements documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Recovery architecture described in SDS Architecture Overview section

controls.title: Compliance with Legal and Contractual Requirements
controls.description: All relevant legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements must be identified and documented for each information system.
requiredEvidence: Applicable legal and regulatory requirements documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Compliance obligations listed with specific regulation references in SRS constraint requirements

controls.title: User Authentication and Password Management
controls.description: User authentication must be controlled through secure management of secret authentication information including passwords.
requiredEvidence: Authentication and password policy requirements documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Authentication mechanism described in SDS Security Architecture — Authentication subsection

controls.title: Physical and Environmental Security
controls.description: Physical security perimeters must be defined and used to protect areas that contain sensitive or critical information and systems.
requiredEvidence: Physical security requirements for hosting environment documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Data centre physical security referenced in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Backup and Recovery
controls.description: Backup copies of information, software, and system images must be taken and tested regularly in accordance with an agreed backup policy.
requiredEvidence: Backup policy and frequency documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Backup and recovery architecture described in SDS Architecture Overview section
Backup testing requirement referenced in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)

controls.title: Change Management
controls.description: Changes to the organisation's systems must be managed through formal change management procedures.
requiredEvidence: Change management procedure referenced in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Deployment and change approval gates described in SDS Component Specifications section

controls.title: Risk Assessment and Treatment
controls.description: Information security risk assessments must be performed at planned intervals and when significant changes occur.
requiredEvidence: Information security risk assessment requirement documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Threat model and key risks described in SDS Security Architecture section
Risk treatment approach referenced in SDS Security Architecture section

controls.title: Data Loss Prevention
controls.description: Data loss prevention measures must be applied to systems, networks, and other devices that process, store, or transmit sensitive information.
requiredEvidence: DLP requirements documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
DLP controls described in SDS Security Architecture — Data Protection subsection
Network egress monitoring referenced in SDS Integration Points section
